executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with at least 100+ Canadian organizations victimized in a single 48-hour period. High-profile targets include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 20 victims, while Clop attacked 15 organizations and Play hit 11. Critical sectors impacted include healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and transportation—indicating coordinated, widespread exploitation likely leveraging a common vulnerability or supply chain compromise.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware assault with 100+ confirmed victims across all provinces in a 48-hour window. Critical infrastructure targeted includes healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Thunder Bay Counselling, Bison Family Medical, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collège Supérieur de Montreal), and public sector (Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and ShinyHunters), Active Green + Ross hit by Sinobi, Kids & Company breached by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (database leaked by Everest ransomware group), Morison Insurance Brokers (Sinobi), Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), SMT Lawyers (Clop)

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for CVE-2026-35616, a vulnerability in Fortinet FortiClientEMS. A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-40175, CVSS 10.0) in the Axios npm library has been disclosed, affecting OpenAI's macOS app signing pipeline and AWS Lambda base images—this appears to be a supply chain compromise linked to North Korean threat actors. The ShinyHunters threat actor claims multiple breaches including Rockstar Games (via Anodot/Snowflake), Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected), and Abrigo Inc. The Qilin ransomware group has made several unverified claims against undisclosed victims, and a new VENOM phishing campaign is actively targeting senior executives' Microsoft credentials.

items.cia_impact: Unknown - details not disclosed in advisory

items.description: Axios npm library vulnerable to remote code execution or complete system compromise; supply chain compromise reaching OpenAI macOS app
items.software_affected: Axios npm library prior to version 1.15.0, OpenAI macOS app, AWS Lambda base images
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Network / exploitation via compromised npm package

items.description: OpenClaw before 2026.3.24 contains an arbitrary code execution vulnerability in local plugin and hook installation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High
items.access_required: Local access required for plugin installation

items.description: Weak password reset in Chamilo LMS allows attackers to hijack accounts using only victim's email
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium
items.access_required: Network / knowledge of victim email address only

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Rockstar Games (via Anodot/Snowflake breach), Wynn Resorts (21,000 employees affected), Abrigo Inc.
items.ttps: T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1530 Data from Cloud Storage Object, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact (ransom demands)

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: Chalmers & Kubeck, Hofland, SAAM Towage (all unverified claims, undisclosed details)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1491 Defacement (leak site postings), T1568 Dynamic Resolution

items.name: MuddyWater (Iranian APT)
items.targets: Middle East targets via Operation Olalampo
items.ttps: T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter (Rust malware), T1102 Web Service (Telegram C2), T1071 Application Layer Protocol

items.name: North Korean threat actors (APT, unspecified group)
items.targets: Axios npm supply chain, OpenAI macOS app signing pipeline
items.ttps: T1195.002 Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain, T1071 Application Layer Protocol, T1027 Obfuscated Files or Information

items.name: Handala (ransomware/hacktivist group)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1491 Defacement

items.name: VENOM (phishing campaign)
items.targets: Senior executives at multiple organizations (5,000+ organizations affected), targeting Microsoft logins
items.ttps: T1566.002 Phishing: Spearphishing Link, T1539 Steal Web Session Cookie, T1078 Valid Accounts

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 on April 7, 2026, warning of CVE-2026-35616 impacting Fortinet FortiClientEMS. No direct incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified in the reporting period. General Canadian topics dominated social feeds but no cyber incidents were reported against CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO, Loblaws, Canadian Tire, or other major Canadian organizations.
